prompt_text: Suggest a fitness plan to build muscle.
response_text:
This is a comprehensive, science-backed muscle-building plan designed for a beginner to intermediate lifter. It focuses on **progressive overload** (gradually increasing weight/reps), **compound movements**, and **proper recovery**.

### The Core Principles
1.  **Train 3-4 days per week.** More is not better; recovery is where muscle grows.
2.  **Lift heavy (for you).** You should struggle to complete the last 1-2 reps of each set.
3.  **Eat in a slight calorie surplus.** You need extra energy to build tissue.
4.  **Eat enough protein.** Aim for **1.6g to 2.2g of protein per kg of body weight** (e.g., a 80kg person needs 128g-176g of protein daily).

---

## The Plan: Full Body (3 Days/Week)

This is the most effective schedule for beginners. It hits every major muscle group 3 times a week.

**Day A (Monday) - Strength Focus**
| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Rest |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Barbell Back Squat | 4 | 5-8 | 90-120 sec |
| Barbell Bench Press | 4 | 6-10 | 90 sec |
| Bent-Over Barbell Row | 4 | 6-10 | 90 sec |
| Dumbbell Shoulder Press | 3 | 8-12 | 60 sec |
| Leg Press (or Lunges) | 3 | 10-15 | 60 sec |
| Plank | 3 | 45-60 sec | 45 sec |

**Day B (Wednesday) - Hypertrophy Focus**
| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Rest |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Deadlift (Conventional or Romanian) | 4 | 5-8 | 120 sec |
| Incline Dumbbell Press | 4 | 8-12 | 60-90 sec |
| Pull-Ups (or Lat Pulldown) | 4 | As many reps as possible (AMRAP) | 90 sec |
| Dumbbell Lateral Raises | 3 | 12-15 | 45 sec |
| Barbell Curl (Biceps) | 3 | 8-12 | 60 sec |
| Triceps Pushdown (or Dips) | 3 | 8-12 | 60 sec |

**Day C (Friday) - Volume & Accessories**
| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Rest |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Goblet Squat (or Front Squat) | 3 | 10-15 | 60 sec |
| Dumbbell Row (One-Arm) | 4 | 8-12 each side | 60 sec |
| Flat Dumbbell Press | 4 | 8-12 | 60 sec |
| Face Pull (Rear Delt & Posture) | 3 | 15-20 | 45 sec |
| Leg Curl (Hamstrings) | 3 | 10-15 | 60 sec |
| Calf Raises | 4 | 15-20 | 45 sec |
| Hanging Leg Raises (Abs) | 3 | 10-15 | 45 sec |

---

## How to Progress (The Most Important Part)

You must increase the challenge over time. Use the **Double Progression** method:
- **Rep Goal:** Aim for the top of the rep range (e.g., 8 reps for a 6-8 set).
- **Add Weight:** Once you can hit the top rep for **all sets**, increase the weight by the smallest increment (e.g., 2.5kg/5lbs) on your next workout.
- **Drop Reps:** You will likely only get 5-6 reps with the new weight. That's fine. Build back up to 8 reps.
- **Repeat.** This is the engine of muscle growth.

---

## Nutrition & Recovery

### Daily Targets (Example for an 80kg person)
- **Calories:** 2,600 - 2,800 (Maintenance + ~300 surplus)
- **Protein:** 140g - 180g (Chicken, eggs, whey, tofu, Greek yogurt)
- **Carbs:** 300g - 350g (Rice, oats, potatoes, fruit)
- **Fats:** 60g - 80g (Olive oil, nuts, avocado)

### Sample Meal Structure (for 150g protein)
- **Breakfast:** 3 eggs + 2 slices whole wheat toast + 1 cup Greek yogurt.
- **Lunch:** 200g chicken breast + 1 cup rice + broccoli.
- **Dinner:** 200g lean beef/ salmon + sweet potato + green beans.
- **Snack:** 1 scoop whey protein + banana + handful of almonds.

### Recovery Non-Negotiables
- **Sleep:** 7-9 hours per night. This is when testosterone and growth hormone peak.
- **Hydration:** 3-4 liters of water daily.
- **Deload Week:** Every 6-8 weeks, do one week with 50% of your normal weight. This prevents burnout and injury.

---

## Common Mistakes to Avoid
1.  **Ego Lifting:** Sacrificing form for weight. You will get injured. Use a weight you can control.
2.  **Skipping Legs:** Squats and deadlifts release the most muscle-building hormones (testosterone, GH). Don't skip them.
3.  **Not Tracking:** Write down your lifts. If you don't know what you did last week, you can't progress.
4.  **Too Much Cardio:** 2-3 sessions of 20-30 min LISS (walking, incline treadmill) is fine. Avoid marathon running while bulking.

**Final Advice:** Stick with this plan for **12 weeks**. Do not change exercises every week. The boring, consistent work is what builds muscle. Take progress photos every 4 weeks to see changes the mirror might miss.
